description_long: This house features a low, angular profile with a modern, geometric design, set into a sloped terrain. The structure appears to be partially embedded in the landscape, with a flat or gently sloping roof and a prominent, dark central section that may represent the main living area or entry. The exterior is predominantly a smooth, concrete-like material, giving it a minimalist and contemporary appearance. There are few visible windows, suggesting a focus on privacy or energy efficiency, and the overall form is asymmetrical with sharp, clean lines. The house is likely a single-story dwelling, with the main entrance located near the central, raised section. The design integrates seamlessly with the surrounding terrain, emphasizing a harmonious relationship with the environment.
description: A modern, single-story house with a low, angular profile and smooth concrete exterior, partially embedded in sloped terrain. The structure features a flat roof and minimal window openings, emphasizing privacy and contemporary design.
description_view_0: From this view, the house's low, angular profile and integration with the sloped terrain are most apparent, with the flat roof and central raised section visible.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the rear or side of the house, showing the depth of the structure and the way it is set into the landscape, with minimal window openings.
description_view_2: Here, the broad, flat expanse of the roof and the asymmetrical shape of the house are visible, along with the main living area at the center.
description_view_3: This perspective emphasizes the elongated, sloping side of the house and the smooth, uninterrupted surface of the exterior walls.
attribute_entryway_design: Minimalist main entry with a flat overhang, no visible porch, and a flush door integrated into the central section.
